Description: Electrical Engineer — power distribution design and IFC drawing production (P.Eng.-stamped)
Notes: Lead engineer: load calc, circuit design, code compliance review, P.Eng. stamp; 84 hrs per LOE

Description: BIM Technician — CAD/BIM drawing production for power distribution plans
Notes: Drawing production: floor plan setup, circuit routing, legend, panel cross-ref sheets; 36 hrs per LOE

Description: Project Manager — coordination and oversight for power distribution plans
Notes: PM oversight: MEP coordination, County submission, schedule tracking; 6 hrs per LOE

Description: Cost Estimator — quantity takeoff and benchmarking support
Notes: Estimate support: load tabulation, cost benchmarking; 4 hrs per LOE

Description: MEP coordination review — cross-discipline coordination with mechanical and structural
Notes: Coordination with PKG-003 (mechanical), PKG-002 (structural), PKG-006 (plumbing); included in engineer hours above

Description: Utility service coordination — confirm three-phase service voltage and capacity
Notes: Coordination with local utility (ATCO Electric or equivalent); included in engineer hours above

Description: County preliminary design submission and approval process
Notes: Submission of power distribution content to County for approval; included in PM and engineer hours above

Description: Code compliance review — Alberta Safety Codes / CEC Part I
Notes: Final code review before P.Eng. stamp; included in engineer hours above
